markets.question: Will Los Angeles Galaxy vs. Vancouver Whitecaps FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
markets.groupItemTitle: Draw (Los Angeles Galaxy vs. Vancouver Whitecaps FC)

events.title: Los Angeles Galaxy vs. Vancouver Whitecaps FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming MLS game, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026 between Los Angeles Galaxy and Vancouver Whitecaps FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vancouver Whitecaps hold a 52.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite, driven by their dominant Western Conference standing at 2nd place with an 8-0-1 record, +20 goal difference, and four straight MLS wins, including a 3-1 victory over Colorado Rapids behind Brian White's brace. LA Galaxy sit 10th at 3-3-4 with defensive vulnerabilities (16 goals conceded in 10 games), tempered by a recent 2-1 home win versus Real Salt Lake fueled by Marco Reus' brace and Joseph Paintsil's return from injury. Both teams face lengthy injury lists—Galaxy missing João Klauss, Matheus Nascimento (OUT), Vancouver without Ryan Gauld, multiple defenders (OUT)—leaving a competitive matchup where LA's home edge and historical head-to-head superiority (18-13-7) keep their 24.5% and draw's 23.5% viable amid Vancouver's road trip start.

markets.question: Will Anthropic have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Mohammed bin Salman cease to be the de facto leader of Saudi Arabia by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia Mohammed bin Salman ceases to be leader of Saudi Arabia for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Mohammed bin Salman's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Mohammed bin Salman and the government of Saudi Arabia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman remains firmly entrenched as Saudi Arabia's de facto leader and prime minister, with consolidated institutional control and no credible internal or external challenges to his position in recent weeks. He chaired a Gulf summit on April 28, hosted Bahrain's king, and led trilateral talks with Jordan and Qatar leaders on regional security, underscoring his active diplomatic role amid Vision 2030's third phase. King Salman's advanced age and prior health episodes in January raise questions about formal succession timing, but no verified reports indicate shifts in the royal line or threats to MBS's authority. Traders monitor for palace intrigue or health developments, though historical precedents favor stability in the Al Saud succession process.

markets.question: Will both candidates advancing to the general election for Governor of California from the June 2, 2026 primary be affiliated with the Republican Party?
markets.description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the listed candidates who advance from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with a party.

In case any combination of parties advances that is not listed here, including all scenarios where an independent candidate advances, this market will resolve to "Other".

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

eventMetadata.context_description: In California's top-two gubernatorial primary on June 2, trader consensus favors a Democrat-Republican matchup advancing at 74.5%, driven by late April polls showing Republican Steve Hilton leading at 16-18% amid a fragmented Democratic field where Tom Steyer (15%), Xavier Becerra (13-14%), and Katie Porter (9-10%) split votes, with 23-26% undecided. Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco trails Rep-Rep hopes at 10-14%, but President Trump's endorsement of Hilton has consolidated GOP support behind him, boosting odds a Democrat secures second place. Eric Swalwell's April 12 campaign suspension amid allegations shifted some Democratic support to Becerra without full consolidation, while an April 22 debate highlighted economic concerns (41% top issue) as ballots near mailing. Dem-Dem odds at 15% reflect persistent liberal vote division, with Rep-Rep at 3.3% underscoring consolidation barriers.

markets.question: Will Ron DeSantis join the Trump administration by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump officially nominates or appoints Ron DeSantis to his administration between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The Trump administration includes individuals formally appointed or nominated by Donald Trump to roles within the U.S. federal government, such as Cabinet members, Executive Office staff, senior policy advisors, ambassadors, or White House staff whose appointments are publicly announced by official government channels.

Nominations will qualify even if the individual is not confirmed.

events.title: Will Ron DeSantis join the Trump administration by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump officially nominates or appoints Ron DeSantis to his administration between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The Trump administration includes individuals formally appointed or nominated by Donald Trump to roles within the U.S. federal government, such as Cabinet members, Executive Office staff, senior policy advisors, ambassadors, or White House staff whose appointments are publicly announced by official government channels.

Nominations will qualify even if the individual is not confirmed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, term-limited out of office in January 2027, faces slim odds of joining the Trump administration by June 30 amid unconfirmed rumors rather than official actions. Recent Axios reporting on April 21 cited sources claiming DeSantis is seeking high-profile roles like Attorney General, Secretary of Defense, or even a Supreme Court seat, prompting Trump to tell reporters on May 1 that he "likes him a lot" and would consider a cabinet spot. However, no nomination, resignation announcement, or formal discussions have materialized in the past week, with DeSantis remaining focused on state duties. Traders' strong 86% No consensus reflects the tight two-month window, historical patterns of early-term cabinet fillings, and absence of momentum for a mid-governorship exit.

markets.question: Will John Thune be the next Senate Majority Leader?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the individual who is announced as the next Senate Majority Leader after the November 3, 2026, U.S. General Election.

This market will remain open until January 3, 2027, at which point it will resolve based on the first announcement of the next Senate Majority Leader from whichever party holds the majority in the U.S. Senate.

If no Majority is established and a leader is not announced by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest for Senate majority control ahead of the November 2026 midterms, with Republicans defending their current 53-47 edge amid competitive battlegrounds like Texas, North Carolina, New Hampshire, Maine, and Ohio. Schumer's slight lead stems from recent polls showing Democratic candidates ahead in key pickups—such as James Talarico over Ken Paxton by 8 points in Texas and Graham Platner over Susan Collins by 9 in Maine—bolstered by April fundraising gains per RacetotheWH models. However, Schumer draws intra-party fire after his recruited Maine Gov. Janet Mills suspended her bid April 30 following internal tensions, fueling doubts on his strategy. Thune benefits from GOP unity and incumbency advantages, but primary dynamics in Georgia and Louisiana keep odds tight; shifts in generic ballot trends or battleground polls could tip the scales.

markets.question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Patrick Mahomes' recovery from a torn ACL/LCL sustained in Week 15 of the 2025 season remains the dominant factor shaping trader consensus, with his 59% implied probability for Chiefs Week 1 starting QB reflecting a standard 9-12 month rehabilitation timeline that introduces meaningful Week 1 risk despite optimistic updates from HC Andy Reid and GM Brett Veach in April. Kansas City restructured Mahomes' contract in February to free $43 million in 2026 cap space while adding quarterback depth via free agency and trades, including Justin Fields (now rostered at #7) at 26%, Gardner Minshew at 27%, and veteran Joe Flacco at 13.7% as potential bridges. Practice squad holdover Chris Oladokun rounds out viable options at 5.5%, underscoring the wisdom-of-crowds hedging against any preseason setback absent recent injury reports or draft additions at the position.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders heavily favor Senator Flávio Bolsonaro at 67.5% implied probability for second place in Brazil's October 4, 2026, presidential election first round, reflecting recent polls showing him surging to challenge President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's lead. April 2026 surveys from AtlasIntel/Bloomberg, Nexus, and Datafolha depict a tight race, with Flávio polling 37-46% in first-round scenarios against Lula's 41-49%, often positioning him as the top right-wing contender amid fragmented opposition fields featuring governors like Romeu Zema, Ronaldo Caiado, and Ratinho Júnior. Lula's 20% odds stem from trader consensus on his first-round plurality, bolstered by incumbency and steady approval, while lower probabilities for Haddad, Santos, and others highlight their weaker polling amid coalition dynamics and no-confidence signals in multi-candidate fragmentation. Upcoming debates and candidate registrations by August could shift vote shares in this closely contested runoff-eligible contest.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US commercial crude oil inventories fell sharply by 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the week ending April 24, per the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, exceeding expectations for a build amid record exports and refinery inputs averaging 16.1 million barrels per day. This follows mixed but net drawdown trends in recent weeks, with gasoline and distillate stocks also declining, signaling robust demand ahead of the summer driving season around Memorial Day. Key factors include steady US production near 13.2 million bpd, import levels, and global dynamics like OPEC+ output decisions; traders watch upcoming Wednesday EIA releases through June 5 for resolution on whether stocks drop below targeted thresholds like 400 million barrels, amid forecasts of seasonal inventory drains.

markets.question: Will D.C. United SC win on 2026-05-03?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 3, 2026
If D.C. United SC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: New York City FC vs. D.C. United SC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming MLS game, scheduled for Sunday, May 3, 2026 between New York City FC and D.C. United SC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a New York City FC win at 56.5% implied probability for their Eastern Conference MLS clash against D.C. United at Yankee Stadium, driven by home advantage and a dominant head-to-head record, including a 3-1 victory in their last meeting. Both sides sit mid-table on 3-3-4 records after 10 matches—NYCFC 7th, D.C. United 10th—separated by goal difference, with NYCFC aiming to snap a recent league winless run following an Open Cup triumph over rivals New York Red Bulls. D.C. United faces absences of center-back Sean Nealis, forward Gabriel Segal, and Hakim Karamoko due to injuries, weakening their already struggling attack, while NYCFC misses suspended full-back Tayvon Gray and injured forward Alonso Martínez, keeping the matchup competitive and elevating draw pricing to 23.5%.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (HIGH) $7.00 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit Week of May 4 2026?
events.description: What will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit Week of May 4 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Opendoor Technologies Inc. The iBuying platform's performance hinges on U.S. housing dynamics, with 30-year mortgage rates easing to 6.23% and forecasts for 8-10% inventory growth supporting potential home sales rebound. Q4 2025 results beat expectations with EPS of -$0.07 versus consensus -$0.10, boosting revenue trends, though persistent losses and competitive pressures cap upside. Traders eye earnings for contribution profit guidance and housing volume metrics, with volatility likely around the post-market report.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Democratic Party candidate Park Soo-hyun at 93% implied probability for the June 3 Chungcheongnam-do gubernatorial election, driven by her dominant performance in the April 26-28 KBS poll showing a 44%-23% lead over incumbent People Power Party Governor Kim Tae-heum across all regions, widening from earlier surveys like TJB's 42%-30% gap. Her April 15 primary runoff win over former Governor Yang Seung-jo consolidated party unity and momentum in this Democratic-leaning province. Park's pledges to advance Daejeon-Chungnam administrative integration and shorten her term for unified 2028 elections align with local growth priorities. Despite the frontrunner status, late scandals, national political shifts, or high turnout among incumbent supporters could narrow the gap before election day.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 17°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nacional welcomes relegation-threatened AVS to Estádio da Madeira in Liga Portugal, where the hosts hold a perfect head-to-head record—three wins and a recent 2-2 draw—with strong home form underpinning trader consensus. Sitting 14th, Nacional gained momentum from a 1-0 victory over Alverca on April 18 despite injuries sidelining midfielders Miguel Baeza and Ulisses plus defender Ivanildo Fernandes. Bottom-placed AVS, winless in recent away league games, salvaged a 2-2 draw at Rio Ave last time out but face desperation in the table amid poor scoring returns. Pre-match fixtures against Tondela and Sporting CP could influence fatigue and lineups.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending AL Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal holds a slim trader edge with a solid 2.70 ERA and 45 strikeouts over 43.1 innings for the Tigers, bolstered by elite control (0.95 WHIP) and back-to-back award pedigree, but Cleveland's breakout lefty Parker Messick (1.73 ERA, 3-0, 38 K) and Angels ace Jose Soriano (0.84 ERA across seven starts, 39 K) have surged into contention via dominant early peripherals in small samples. Bryce Miller, Dylan Cease (high-K TOR newcomer), and Yankees prospect Cam Schlittler add depth with sub-3.00 ERAs and quality starts, reflecting widespread rotation health and no major injury reports keeping the market tightly contested amid April's volatility and full schedules ahead.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Austin be between 52-53°F on May 4?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors 66°F or higher (99.6%) for Austin's highest temperature on May 4, backed by strong alignment in National Weather Service guidance and ensemble outputs from GFS and ECMWF models projecting highs in the mid-70s to low-80s°F. This positioning stems from the past 48 hours' forecast convergence on a high-pressure ridge promoting sunny skies, light southerly winds, and efficient daytime heating after recent wetter conditions eased. Climatological norms reinforce this, with historical May 4 highs averaging near 83°F. Realistic challenges include unexpected convective showers boosting cloud cover or a surprise cool front, though upper-air patterns indicate minimal risk; watch NWS Austin/San Antonio's next 12Z updates for refinements.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Illinois' 9th Congressional District, a longtime Democratic stronghold with a partisan voting index exceeding D+20, favors Democratic nominee Daniel Biss following his victory in the crowded March 17 primary over 15 challengers, solidifying party support in the wake of Rep. Jan Schakowsky's retirement. Biss enters the November 3 general election with strong fundraising—over $250,000 banked in early April—and endorsements, contrasting weak Republican nominee John Elleson, a pastor with limited resources in a district last won by a GOP candidate decades ago. Trader consensus prices a Democratic victory at 92.5%, reflecting historical margins and low GOP turnout. Upsets remain possible via a major Biss scandal, national Republican midterm wave, or voting irregularities, though barriers are high.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Mets head to Angel Stadium for the middle game of their series against the Los Angeles Angels after rallying for a 4-3 win in Friday's opener, powered by Ronny Mauricio's go-ahead seventh-inning home run that snapped their road losing streak. Both clubs sit dead last in their divisions—Mets at 11-21 (NL East), Angels 12-21 (AL West)—amid slumps, with the Angels dropping seven of eight before the loss and just 1-9 in their last 10. Probable starters Nolan McLean (Mets: 1.46 road ERA, 0.57 WHIP) and Reid Detmers (Angels) face pressure from depleted rotations; Mets miss Francisco Lindor (calf, weeks out) and Kodai Senga (back inflammation), while Angels lack catcher Logan O'Hoppe (wrist, 10-day IL) and pitchers like Yusei Kikuchi (shoulder). Jorge Soler's 21-game on-base streak and AL-top-5 25 RBIs provide Angels' lone offensive spark.

markets.question: Will Williams Companies (WMB) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Williams Companies is estimated to release earnings on May 4, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Williams Companies’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.63 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Williams Companies reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.63 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Williams Companies releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: Will François Ruffin win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Jordan Bardella leads trader consensus at 22% implied probability for the 2027 French presidential election, just ahead of Édouard Philippe at 19.5%, reflecting post-municipal election polls from late March 2026 where Bardella tops first-round intentions at 34-38% amid Rassemblement National gains in local races and widespread voter desire for deep political change. Philippe, bolstered by his strong Le Havre mayoral reelection, edges Bardella in runoff hypotheticals (51.5-52% to 48-48.5%), highlighting a fragmented field that favors RN's first-round strength but leaves the ultimate outcome contested. The race stays tight due to undecided nominations, potential center-right consolidation, and no dominant second-round barrier, with key shifts possible from party primaries or economic developments before April 2027 voting.

markets.question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 77.5% implied probability for an Iranian agent—such as an IRGC operative or spy engaged in espionage or assassination plots—being charged by the Department of Justice by May 31, reflecting the absence of qualifying DOJ or FBI indictments in recent weeks. The March conviction of Pakistani national Asif Merchant in an Iran-linked murder-for-hire scheme targeting Donald Trump marked the last major such case, but no new charges against Iranian intelligence personnel have followed. Recent actions, including the April 19 arrest of Iranian national Shamim Mafi at LAX for sanctions violations tied to brokering arms sales to Sudan and the April 25 indictment of Jafar Tafakori for migrant smuggling, involve economic crimes rather than explicit agency allegations. Amid heightened U.S. sanctions on Iran weapons networks and ongoing regional tensions, including a naval blockade, traders see insufficient momentum for a qualifying development before the deadline.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,000 (HIGH) in June?
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 2026 parliamentary elections, which produced a hung parliament under proportional representation, incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement conceded on April 20 its inability to secure a coalition majority after failed negotiations. President Nataša Pirc Musar urged lawmakers on April 25 to nominate a candidate able to form a stable government, positioning Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) and allies as frontrunners with the strongest path to 46 votes in the 90-seat National Assembly. Janša's recent insistence on avoiding weak coalitions, coupled with reports of ministry reductions signaling progress in talks, drives trader consensus favoring him at 88.6% implied probability, while alternatives like Jernej Vrtovec trail due to narrower alliance prospects; a surprise left-leaning pact or deadlock could still shift dynamics ahead of a confidence vote.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Senator Lindsey Graham's commanding trader consensus at 86.5% in the South Carolina Republican Senate primary reflects his incumbency advantages, including a March 2025 endorsement from Donald Trump, Governor Henry McMaster's support as campaign chair, and a fundraising lead exceeding $20 million. Challenger Mark Lynch holds 12.6% after Paul Dans's April 10 withdrawal and endorsement consolidated some anti-Graham sentiment, yet recent polls like NYT's 43%-29% Graham lead and neutral surveys showing Graham ahead 41%-21% underscore persistent field fragmentation with Thomas Murphy at 0.1%. High Graham unfavorables and self-funded Lynch attacks on foreign policy have narrowed the gap slightly, but historical SC GOP primary patterns favor three-time incumbents ahead of the June 9 contest.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Atlanta be 80°F or higher on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ga/atlanta/KATL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts from the Peachtree City office project a high near 70°F in Atlanta on May 3 under mostly sunny skies, following morning showers on May 2 that ushered in a cool front with drier northerly flow and radiational cooling overnight to lows near 43°F. This post-frontal setup has positioned 70-71°F (39% implied probability) and 68-69°F (28.5%) as leading outcomes, about 9°F below the late-spring climatological average of 79°F, with model ensembles like GFS and NAM showing tight agreement on limited recovery due to lingering upper-level trough influences. Trader sentiment aggregates this consensus, though minor uncertainties in boundary-layer mixing or afternoon heating could nudge peaks toward 72-73°F (24%); final resolution awaits official observations from Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport after 11:59 PM EDT May 2.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the #3 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Girona qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Champions League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 LaLiga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Champions League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or LaLiga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Jerry Nadler, trader consensus slightly favors Assemblyman Micah Lasher at 40.5% over Alex Bores at 34%, with Jack Schlossberg at 18% amid a fragmented 18-candidate field splitting votes in this safe Democratic seat. Mixed internal polls—Bores' April survey showing Schlossberg at 22% and himself at 19%, Lasher at 14%; earlier Conway poll with Schlossberg leading—highlight high undecideds and competing lanes: Lasher buoyed by Gov. Kathy Hochul's April 13 endorsement, Bores resilient with progressive backing as Working Families Party rejects Schlossberg (May 1) over experience gaps. The race stays tight due to ideological divides and older voters potentially dominating turnout; separation could come from union endorsements, FEC fundraising reports, or public polls.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Shadow Secretary" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

events.title: What will Keir Starmer say at the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
events.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors Keir Starmer referencing "Mr. Speaker" 10+ times (97%) at the next Prime Minister's Questions after the May 13 State Opening, based on his frequent use to manage rowdy exchanges with Conservative Leader Kemi Badenoch. High implied probabilities for NHS mentions (71%) reflect ongoing healthcare debates, while anti-Semitism/anti-Semitic (60%) and Jewish (45%) terms track recent community tensions post-Golders Green attack and Gaza protests. Last week's April 29 PMQs spotlighted energy shortages from the Iran conflict, cost-of-living pressures, and welfare amid recession warnings; expected Labour setbacks in May 7 local elections will likely intensify opposition scrutiny on economic delivery when Parliament reconvenes around May 20.

markets.question: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Madrid, entrenched in fourth place in the La Liga table after 33 matches, host sixth-placed RC Celta de Vigo on May 9 at Riyadh Air Metropolitano in a pivotal late-season clash for Champions League qualification. Los Colchoneros' recent form includes gritty home wins over Athletic Bilbao (3-2) and Getafe (1-0), bolstered by strong head-to-head dominance (19 wins in 30 meetings), though injuries to José Giménez (muscle), Dávid Hancko (ankle), and Pablo Barrios (thigh) temper squad depth. Celta, winless in their last five with a 1-0 loss to Barcelona, face away form woes despite a 1-1 draw at home earlier this season; key absences like Manu Fernández (muscle) add pressure amid their Europa League push. Home advantage and table stakes shape trader consensus.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 81.5% implied probability against a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting the FOMC's April 29 decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% for a third straight meeting despite March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year from energy price shocks. The March dot plot median projects a slight end-2026 rate of 3.4%, signaling expectations of stability or modest easing amid cooling labor market signals, with futures markets implying a steady path near 3.6%. Heightened geopolitical oil risks prompted some FOMC openness to hikes in minutes, but traders view inflation as transitory. Key catalysts include April CPI data due May 12 and the June 17-18 FOMC meeting.

markets.question: Jimmy Lai released by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Lai is released from custody by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Lai is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Lai is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Lai to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Extradition of Lai to another country where he remains in custody will also not count.

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong courts convicted pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai in December 2025 on national security law charges including conspiracy to collude with foreign forces and sedition, sentencing him to 20 years' imprisonment on February 9, 2026—the harshest penalty yet under the law imposed by Beijing. His legal team confirmed in early March that he will not appeal, solidifying his detention with no procedural path to release by June 30 amid strict enforcement of the national security framework. Trader consensus at 97.5% "No" reflects this finality, though his age (77) and reported health decline could prompt humanitarian considerations, alongside diplomatic pressures during upcoming US-China summits like Trump-Xi talks.

markets.question: Will Willy Adames lead the MLB in doubles for the 2026 regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the player who hits the most doubles during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player with the higher batting average during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the player with the higher slugging percentage during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the player that records more hits during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If no 2026 Michigan Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Michigan Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Perry Johnson as the Republican primary frontrunner for Michigan governor at 49% implied probability, ahead of U.S. Rep. John James at 36%, diverging from late-April Glengariff/Detroit Regional Chamber polling showing James leading 37% to Johnson's 20% among likely voters. Johnson's self-funding exceeding $15 million fuels market bets on an advertising surge in the crowded August 4 primary field, where Mike Cox trails at 10-18% and others like Aric Nesbitt fragment support. Recent signature validations confirmed ballot access for top contenders, amid a lawsuit by Johnson's campaign against James over ad claims, heightening pre-primary tensions ahead of potential debates.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: In New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election on April 16, certified results show Democrat Analilia Mejia defeating Republican Joe Hathaway by a 20.82% margin (60.18% to 39.36%, plus 0.46% third-party), with 28,305-vote lead from over 136,000 ballots—far exceeding typical special election turnout. This commanding trader consensus stems from stable final tallies across Essex, Morris, Passaic, Sussex, and Warren counties, where Mejia outperformed 2024 Democratic benchmarks by double digits in diverse suburbs amid high early voting. Mejia's progressive platform and fundraising edge bolstered her hold on the seat vacated by Gov. Mikie Sherrill. Barring improbable shifts from cured provisionals or recounts, the outcome aligns firmly with the 20-25% bin.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Metamask's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the FL-23 Democratic primary slightly favors challenger Oliver Adams Larkin at 35.5% over incumbent Rep. Jared Moskowitz at 26%, driven by Larkin's recent national endorsement from the Democratic Socialists of America on March 31 and grassroots momentum criticizing Moskowitz's pro-Israel stance amid Gaza tensions and limited constituent town halls. Moskowitz counters with superior fundraising—leading quarters like Q1 2026—and incumbency advantages in the Broward-Palm Beach district. Conflicting polls, including a March survey showing Moskowitz at 45% versus Larkin's internals claiming leads after balanced bios, underscore the tight race. Recent redistricting finalized in late April has stabilized lines, but upcoming debates, Q2 fundraising reports, and independent polling could create separation ahead of the August 18 primary.

events.title: Largest Company end of May?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the largest company in the world by market cap on May 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors NVIDIA at 79.4% implied probability to hold the title of world's largest company by market capitalization at May's end, anchored by its current ~$5 trillion valuation driven by unrelenting demand for AI GPUs amid data center buildouts and upcoming Vera Rubin chip shipments. Alphabet trails at 20.1% after surging with a record $421 billion market cap addition over the past two days, fueled by robust Google Cloud growth and AI service momentum that recently overtook Apple for second place at ~$4.2 trillion. The tight race reflects Alphabet's potential to close the ~15-20% gap before month-end, while others like Apple (0.7%) lag further due to slower AI positioning; watch NVIDIA's mid-May earnings for volatility.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) settle over $75 on the final trading day of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Silver futures (SI) have rallied sharply to around $76 per ounce as of May 2, 2026, up over 3% in the latest session amid U.S. dollar depreciation and softer oil prices, bolstering industrial demand from solar panels, EVs, and electronics amid the Silver Institute's forecast of a near-record supply deficit. Trader consensus reflects bullish sentiment, with J.P. Morgan projecting an $81 average for 2026 driven by green energy transition and inflation-hedge flows, though a stronger dollar or hawkish Fed rhetoric could cap gains. Key catalysts ahead include May CPI data (June 11 release), June FOMC meeting (June 17-18), and nonfarm payrolls, which could influence rate cut expectations and USD dynamics through end-June settlement.

Jordan Spieth, Scottie Scheffler, Nick Taylor, and Alex Smalley sit T2 at eight-under, while a packed T5-T15 group at five- to seven-under—featuring Gary Woodland, Si Woo Kim, and Alex Fitzpatrick—bolsters their positions in the no-cut signature event. Young's bogey-free Friday surge and strong course history contrast challengers' recent momentum, like Fitzpatrick's six-under 66, though Blue Monster's demanding layout and hole-18 trouble could spark movement over the weekend.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atalanta BC holds a clear edge as trader consensus favorite at 63.5% implied probability for their Serie A home clash against Genoa CFC at Gewiss Stadium, driven by their seventh-place standing with 54 points and urgent need for a win to bolster Conference League qualification amid a four-game winless streak. Genoa, sitting 14th on 39 points and virtually safe from relegation, faces motivation challenges and key absences including Caleb Ekuban's muscle injury and Brooke Norton-Cuffy's hamstring issue, limiting their upset chances at 14.5%. The draw at 21.5% reflects balanced historical head-to-head records (39 wins apiece in 109 meetings) and Atalanta's recent dip, though near-full strength with Ederson and Scamacca leading supports home dominance.

markets.question: Will Pixelworks (PXLW) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Pixelworks is estimated to release earnings on May 12, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Pixelworks’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.14 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Pixelworks reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $-0.14 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Pixelworks releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 74% implied probability against the U.S. enacting a comprehensive AI safety bill before 2027, anchored by persistent congressional gridlock in the 119th Congress despite the Trump administration's March 20, 2026, National AI Legislative Framework urging light-touch federal rules, state preemption, and innovation-focused measures like child safety protections. Dozens of bills—such as the AI Guardrails Act (S.4113) and recent bipartisan proposals targeting kids' AI chatbot use—remain stalled in committees amid partisan divides, appropriations battles, and competing priorities. States like California (SB 1047, 2025) and New York (RAISE Act, December 2025) have filled the void with enacted safety laws, underscoring federal inaction. Lame-duck session dynamics offer slim prospects for passage given historical challenges in tech regulation.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

Navalnaya's edge draws from her inherited anti-Putin activism narrative, echoing past laureates like dissidents; Zelenskyy's from symbolic wartime leadership alongside Ukraine's people; and Trump's from speculation over diplomatic breakthroughs, including a purported Israel-Hamas ceasefire. No outcome exceeds 8%, highlighting intense competition amid the committee's opaque deliberations—key swings hinge on fresh humanitarian crises, precursor endorsements, or geopolitical resolutions before the October 9 announcement.

markets.question: Manchester United FC vs. Liverpool FC: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game between Manchester United FC and Liverpool FC, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Manchester United FC and Liverpool FC combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Madrid be 22°C on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit (HIGH) $90 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Claude go down 12+ times in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of days in the month of May during which Claude (claude.ai) experiences any downtime.

Claude will be considered to have experienced downtime on a given day if the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/) displays that day’s status box as any color other than green once the day’s status box is finalized. Daily status boxes for Claude components other than claude.ai will not impact this market’s resolution.

A day will be considered finalized once the following day’s status box has a display color other than the grey color used for days which have not yet begun.

This market will resolve once the final day in May 2026 is finalized. If the final day in May 2026 has not been finalized by June 7, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the days in May which have been finalized up to that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/).
